pet.name: MOMO
pet.characteristics: Squirrels require large, multi-level enclosures with climbing structures, branches, and nesting boxes to support natural behavior. Diet consists of nuts, seeds, vegetables, and fruits; feeding should occur daily with fresh water always available. They are highly active and need substantial enrichment including foraging opportunities, chew toys, and environmental variety to prevent stress and stereotypic behavior. Handling should be minimal and gentle, as squirrels are prey animals and easily stressed. A three-limbed squirrel may experience mobility challenges; monitor for compensatory strain, ensure ramps and lower perches are accessible, and watch for signs of pain or infection at the injury site. Regular veterinary check-ups are essential, particularly to assess healing and prevent secondary complications.
pet.species_profile_summary: Captive squirrels typically live 8-15 years with proper nutrition, spacious enriched housing, and veterinary monitoring; limb loss requires adapted environment and close observation for complications.

age_context.summary: Captive squirrels typically live 8-15 years with proper nutrition, spacious enriched housing, and veterinary monitoring; limb loss requires adapted environment and close observation for complications.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Observation tasks are useful for special-needs pets because they create a record of appetite, water intake, energy, and bathroom habits. These tasks should be short, specific, and easy to complete consistently.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: A senior pet plan should prioritize consistency over intensity. When time is limited, essential care such as feeding, hydration, medication reminders, and mobility support should be scheduled before optional enrichment.

retrieved_passages.title: Kidney Diet and Hydration Support
retrieved_passages.content: Owners should follow the veterinary feeding plan already prescribed and avoid changing food or supplement amounts without veterinary guidance. If a pet refuses multiple meals or shows sudden decline, the safe action is to contact a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Older pets may benefit from routines that include medication reminders, slow feeding, and extra time for transitions. Owners should watch for changes in gait, confusion, appetite, and bathroom habits and report major changes to a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Daily care plans are stronger when they combine essential care with realistic enrichment and observation. Short play, sniff walks, brushing, or calm interaction can improve quality of life without requiring large blocks of time.

raw_recommendations.name: Daily appetite and intake monitoring
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe and record MOMO's food consumption, water intake, and energy level. Note any changes from baseline. MOMO is currently losing appetite, so consistent daily observation is critical.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Appetite loss in senior pets requires close tracking to detect early decline. Observation tasks create a record for veterinary consultation and help identify patterns.

raw_recommendations.name: Fresh water availability check
raw_recommendations.notes: Ensure fresh water is always available in MOMO's enclosure. Check water container is clean and filled daily.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Consistent hydration is essential for senior pets and supports overall health. Daily checks ensure water remains fresh and accessible.

raw_recommendations.name: Feeding with veterinary plan adherence
raw_recommendations.notes: Provide daily meals following the established veterinary feeding plan. Do not change food amounts or introduce new foods without veterinary guidance. Monitor if MOMO refuses meals.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior pets benefit from consistent feeding routines. Appetite loss requires strict adherence to prescribed diet and immediate veterinary contact if refusal continues.

raw_recommendations.name: Mobility and limb site observation
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe MOMO's movement and balance on three limbs. Check for signs of compensatory strain, pain, or infection at the limb loss site. Note any changes in mobility or gait.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Three-limbed senior squirrels require daily monitoring for secondary complications and strain. Early detection of pain or infection prevents serious complications.

raw_recommendations.name: Accessible perch and ramp verification
raw_recommendations.notes: Verify that lower perches, ramps, and climbing structures remain accessible and stable for three-limbed movement. Ensure no obstacles block easy access to food, water, and nesting areas.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Adapted environment is essential for three-limbed senior squirrels. Weekly checks ensure mobility aids remain functional and safe.

raw_recommendations.name: Gentle enrichment and calm interaction
raw_recommendations.notes: Provide low-stress enrichment such as accessible foraging opportunities, soft chew materials, or gentle environmental variety. Keep interaction calm and brief to avoid stress.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior pets benefit from realistic enrichment that improves quality of life without intensity. Calm interaction supports mental health while respecting MOMO's prey-animal nature.

raw_recommendations.name: Bathroom habits and waste observation
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe and note MOMO's bathroom habits weekly. Report any changes in frequency, consistency, or appearance to veterinarian.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Changes in bathroom habits are early indicators of health decline in senior pets and should be tracked and reported.

raw_recommendations.name: Veterinary consultation reminder for appetite loss
raw_recommendations.notes: Contact veterinarian if appetite loss continues or MOMO refuses multiple meals. Provide observation notes from daily monitoring.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Sudden appetite decline in senior pets requires professional evaluation. Safe action is veterinary contact when refusal pattern emerges.

blocked_recommendations.name: Feeding with veterinary plan adherence
reasons: Recommendation contains unsafe or unsupported medical advice.
